game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Infinifactory is a sandbox puzzle game by Zachtronics, the creators of SpaceChem and Infiniminer. Build factories that assemble products for your alien overlords, and try not to die in the process. Infinifactory is a sandbox puzzle game by Zachtronics, the creators of SpaceChem and Infiniminer. Build factories that assemble products for your alien overlords, and try not to die in the process.

  • LIKE SPACECHEM… IN 3D! Design and run factories in a first-person, fully 3D environment.
  • HISTOGRAMS ARE BACK! Optimize your solutions, and then optimize them more when you see how much better your friends did.
  • VISIT EXOTIC ALIEN LOCALES! Explore a story-driven campaign with 50+ puzzles, audio logs, and more.
  • BLOCKS THAT MOVE! Go beyond the campaign and push the limits of Infinifactory’s next-generation block engine in the sandbox.
  • STEAM WORKSHOP INTEGRATION! Create, share, and play custom puzzles on Steam Workshop.
game_info.description: Welcome to FactoryBlocks! You're a factory engineer building automated production lines for alien overlords. Place conveyor belts and processors to transform raw materials into required products. Complete increasingly complex manufacturing challenges while managing limited space and resources. Master the art of efficient factory design to survive!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate and move cursor in build mode
Space: Place/Remove selected component
Z: Cycle through available components (Conveyor, Processor, Rotator)
Shift: Toggle build mode on/off
Enter: Start game from menu
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart and return to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic factory building and production. Validates that the player can place conveyor belts, spawn materials, and process them to complete a simple level objective.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Place a simple conveyor line from spawner to goal. Add necessary processors to transform raw materials into the target product type. Wait for materials to flow through and reach the goal area.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Materials should spawn, move along conveyors, get processed correctly, and reach the goal. The level should complete successfully with GAME_OVER_WIN state achieved.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests optimal factory design and winning strategy. Implements an efficient layout that completes the objective quickly with minimal components.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze the current level requirements. Place an optimized conveyor network with strategically positioned processors. Create direct paths from spawners through processors to goals.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Win condition achieved efficiently. All required products delivered to goal zones. Game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state demonstrating mastery of factory mechanics.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests component placement and removal mechanics. Validates that all component types can be placed, removed, and interact correctly with materials.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Cycle through all available components using Z key. Place each component type in different grid positions. Remove and replace components to test editing functionality.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: All component types place correctly. Materials interact properly with each component. Removal works without errors. No collision or placement bugs occur.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests complex factory routing with multiple processors. Validates that materials can be processed multiple times in sequence to create complex products.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Build a multi-stage factory with conveyors routing materials through multiple processors in sequence. Test various processor arrangements and material flow patterns.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Materials successfully flow through multi-stage processing. Each processor transforms materials correctly. Final products match requirements and reach goals.
